title: Beryl Chiffonage
content:

{{Otheruses|Beryl}}

{{Quote|Rogue Group, use your harpoons and tow cables. Go for the legs. It might be our only chance of stopping them.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="Episode 5">{{Film|V}}</ref>}}
'''Beryl Chiffonage''' was a [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. An expert military tactician with an intimate knowledge of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] battle tactics, Chiffonage worked closely with [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] and [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]] as a member of the [[Task Force on Alliance Security]]. Under the leadership of Alliance [[General/Legends|Generals]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] and [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]], Chiffonage helped to devise attack and defensive maneuvers for Rebel bases, including the [[Dantooine base/Legends|base]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] and [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]].

Chiffonage helped train tactical prodigy [[Risiev Credal]] in a variety of ground and airborne scenarios. Before Credal inexplicably disappeared from the Alliance, considered by High Command and Intelligence to be a traitor, he and Chiffonage, in cooperation with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], developed a series of tactics for [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeder]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] to be utilized against Imperial [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] as part of Echo Base's defensive measures. Known as the [[Rogue Doctrine]], Chiffonage's innovation resulted specifically in [[Attack Pattern Delta/Legends|Attack Pattern Delta]] and a maneuver in which the [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]]' [[Power harpoon launcher|harpoon and tow cables]] entangled and tripped the [[Walker/Legends|walkers]]' legs.

Skywalker and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Group]] employed Chiffonage's tactics at the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] against the invading line of AT-ATs with limited success, downing several walkers throughout the engagement. Chiffonage was captured trying to flee Hoth when his escape [[Transport/Legends|transport]] was stopped at an Imperial [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|checkpoint]].

==Biography==
===Alliance High Command===
[[File:Airencracken.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Airen Cracken, who Chiffonage worked closely with]]
Beryl Chiffonage was a noted military tactician of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Assault on Hoth">''[[Assault on Hoth]]''</ref> His<ref name="Who's Who" /> service with the Rebellion dated back to the earliest [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the Alliance's struggles against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. His duties and assignment locales were typically kept confidential in accordance with his close work with [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]].<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref>

Chiffonage's High Command duties regularly included helping to devise offensive and defensive strategic patterns for a particular Rebel base, working alongside notable Alliance leadership figures such as [[General/Legends|Generals]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] and [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]]. He also worked closely with [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]] under [[Chief of Intelligence/Legends|Chief of Intelligence]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] as part of the [[Task Force on Alliance Security]], compiling reports on individuals posing significant threats to the Alliance.<ref name="AIR" /> As of [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="Date note">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' is set one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], which is in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. This places ''Alliance Intelligence Reports'' as being set in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]].</ref> Chiffonage, a [[Lieutenant Colonel/Legends|lieutenant colonel]], was instrumental in the execution of several Alliance operations, including the [[Engagement at Jestan]].<ref name="AIR" /> At some point before the end of 2 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],<ref name="Date note" /> Chiffonage had a run-in with [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] on the astronomical object [[Liaq]].<ref name="AIR" />

===Risiev Credal and the Rogue Doctrine===
Chiffonage was stationed at High Command's [[Dantooine base/Legends|headquarters]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] when [[Risiev Credal]], an up-and-coming tactical prodigy in his own right, was transferred to the base. For some [[Time/Legends|time]] Chiffonage trained Credal, who was considered to be one of the Alliance's future leaders, in tactics for a countless variety of scenarios, including the [[Dsalisn Theories]] on low [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] combat and the urban tactics used on<ref name="AIR" /> the planet<ref name="Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-2-ancient-coruscant|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 2 - Ancient Coruscant}}</ref> [[Thyrsus/Legends|Thyrsus]]. Credal's work reflected the expert tutelage under which he learned.<ref name="AIR" />

[[File:PowerHarpoon.png|thumb|right|300px|The power harpoon, originally designed by Beryl Chiffonage]]
Chiffonage and Credal, together with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Rogue Leader/Legends|leader]] of the elite [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], developed a series of innovative tactical maneuvers for [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeder]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] in the event of attack from Imperial [[Walker/Legends|walkers]].<ref name="AIR" /> Known as the "[[Rogue Doctrine]]," which consisted of three individual strategic components,<ref name="RAS">[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'', Second Edition]]</ref> drawn together from the collective experience of all Rogue Squadron [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] over the years,<ref name="Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 10">{{StarshipsVehiclesCite|10|Action Stations}}</ref> the trio's efforts set the standard for combat [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]] tactics. Specifically, Chiffonage and Credal devised [[Attack Pattern Delta/Legends|Attack Pattern Delta]], a veritable game of "chicken," in which a group of speeders approached a walker in single-line formation in order to give the walker a single target at which to fire. While the walker could only track a single speeder at a time, the other speeders were offered a clear shot and a clean getaway.<ref name="RAS" />

But perhaps the most difficult element of the doctrine to execute, as devised by Chiffonage and Skywalker,<ref name="RAS" /> was inspired by a group of primitives. Chiffonage realized that walkers were too large and well-armed for most Rebel weaponry, so he dreamed of a way to turn the walkers' greatest strength—their size—into their greatest weakness. Chiffonage knew that the walkers could potentially be crushed under their own weight if the Rebels could only find a way to trip them. During an expedition to the primitive planet [[Verig]], Chiffonage found his answer in Verig's [[Unidentified Verig species|native nomadic hunters]], who used a [[Bola/Legends|bola]]-like weapon, a long [[Rope/Legends|rope]] with stones tied to each end, to trip the giant, [[Elephant/Legends|elephantine]] [[Pryss-creature]]s. Drawing upon what he learned from Verig,<ref name="Assault on Hoth" /> as well as previous Rebel operations on the planets [[Battle of Corellia (1 ABY)|Corellia]] and [[Raid on Balmorra|Balmorra]], in which Rebel pilots improvised a way to trip advancing [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] by using the tow cables of T-47 airspeeders, a last-ditch technique,<ref name="Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 10" /> Chiffonage personally designed a special high-powered harpoon attached to a [[flexisteel]] tow cable and [[Fusion disk|fusion disc]] for use in a unique tactical maneuver of his own. Attached to the rear of the T-47, the resultant [[Power harpoon launcher|harpoon and tow cable]] would be used to tangle a walker's legs and trip it to the ground, effectively neutralizing the war machine.<ref name="RAS" /> For reasons neither High Command nor Intelligence could begin to understand,<ref name="AIR" /> in 2 ABY,<ref name="Date note" /> Credal jumped into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] while overseeing patrols in the [[Anoat system/Legends|Anoat system]], never to be heard from again. Although Intelligence believed him to be a traitor, Chiffonage filed a report for the Task Force on Alliance Security regarding Credal, praising his contributions and expressing his expectations for Credal's eventual return.<ref name="AIR" />

===Battle of Hoth===
{{Quote|That armor's too strong for blasters.|Luke Skywalker, realizing the ineffectiveness of Attack Pattern Delta during the Battle of Hoth|<ref name="Episode 5" />}}
[[File:SnowspeederCCG.png|thumb|left|250px|Rebel snowspeeders employ Chiffonage's Attack Pattern Delta at the Battle of Hoth.]]
As of [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> Chiffonage, since promoted to [[Commander/Legends|commander]], served with High Command under Rieekan at the secret [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the planet [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], and was regularly stationed in the base's command center as a controller. He was present in the command center, working with [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], when [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] reported to Rieekan of his need to leave the Alliance in order to settle personal issues.<ref name="Who's Who" />

When an Imperial [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droid]] discovered Echo Base shortly thereafter, Rieekan ordered an immediate evacuation of the facility. Chiffonage and Skywalker knew the Alliance would be facing Imperial walkers in the imminent engagement, and to that end they planned to utilize the tactics of Rogue Doctrine with the base's modified T-47s, known as "[[Snowspeeder/Legends|snowspeeders]]," to muster what offense they could in [[Evac Rearguard Action 101|delaying]] the Imperial attack. During the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Skywalker and Rogue Group discovered that the thick [[Armor/Legends|armored]] hulls of the Imperial AT-ATs were impervious to their speeders' [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]], nullifying whatever effects Attack Pattern Delta may have had. Instead, Skywalker ordered the Rogues to use their harpoons and tow cables to trip the walkers' legs in a last-ditch effort to stop their advance.<ref name="GG3">[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]]</ref>

Although the Rebellion suffered a major defeat at Hoth, Chiffonage's designed maneuvers resulted in the downing of several AT-ATs during the battle,<ref name="Who's Who" /> the success of which the Rebels owed directly to the nomads of Verig.<ref name="Assault on Hoth" /> While a significant portion of Echo Base's command staff succeeded in evacuating the planet and slipping by the Imperial [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], Chiffonage was captured when his escape [[Transport/Legends|transport]] attempted to pass through an Imperial [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|checkpoint]].<ref name="Who's Who" />

===Tow cable legacy===
[[File:HarpoonTowCable-SW40.png|thumb|right|250px|As a testament to Chiffonage's developed tactics, a snowspeeder uses a tow cable to entangle the legs of an AT-AT on Hoth.]]
Although Chiffonage was credited with coming up with the idea to use tow cables to trip the AT-AT walkers on Hoth,<ref name="Who's Who" /> a young Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] recruit, [[Davin Felth/Legends|Davin Felth]], hypothesized a similar maneuver<ref name="Desert Winds">{{StoryCite|story=When the Desert Wind Turns: The Stormtrooper's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref> years before in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> during an AT-AT training exercise. Felth suggested to then-[[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]], head of the Imperial AT-AT program, that the walkers' legs were particularly vulnerable to being tangled and tripped with a cable. While Felth's discovery greatly impressed Veers, he kept the information private and buried Felth in the Imperial [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] to prevent the secret from damaging his career, which was closely linked to the success of the walkers.<ref name="Desert Winds" /> Ironically, Veers would face Chiffonage's identical tactic years later at Hoth, to somewhat devastating effects.<ref name="GG3" />

[[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], the first pilot to down an AT-AT at Hoth using the maneuver, would reuse the tactic several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] later to trip three more walkers at the [[Battle of Kothlis]]. Rogue Squadron helped to secure the [[Death Star II plans|plans]] to the Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] at that engagement.<ref name="Rogue Leader">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Airen and I would spent [sic] a great deal of time prior to the meetings recounting the stories of earlier times to the ever-eager Credal, and even if no tactics or lessons were intended, he soaked it all up. When he returns from wherever he has gone, I'm going to harshly reprimand him…then tell him about the time I ran into those mercenaries on Liaq.|Excerpt from Beryl Chiffonage's report to Alliance Intelligence, concerning Risiev Credal|<ref name="AIR" />}}
Prior to Alliance military briefings, Beryl Chiffonage would spend a great amount of time reminiscing about his early career with Airen Cracken and Credal, who would eagerly and intently listen to the stories, whether or not there were tactical lessons to be learned. Even after Credal abandoned the Alliance, although Chiffonage expressed that he would harshly reprimand his student, he also wished to tell him about his encounter with mercenaries on Liaq.<ref name="AIR" /> He was a fair-skinned [[Human/Legends|Human]], with [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]].<ref name="Who's Who" />

==Skills and abilities==
Beryl Chiffonage displayed tactical brilliance during his career developing strategy for Alliance High Command. His abilities earned him assignments to the Rebel headquarters on Dantooine and Hoth, and he contributed to the success of several Alliance operations as well as helping to train the talented Risiev Credal.<ref name="AIR" /> Chiffonage's intimate knowledge of Imperial battle tactics was crucial in developing the Rogue Doctrine with Credal and Luke Skywalker,<ref name="RAS" /> and he personally designed the harpoon and tow cable, with inspiration from Verig's primitive natives<ref name="Assault on Hoth" /> and former Rogue Squadron operations,<ref name="Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 10" /> that would be attached to the rear of Echo Base's T-47 airspeeders for use by Rebel [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]]<ref name="RAS" /> as a reserve defense against Imperial walkers.<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

==Equipment==
During his service [[Time/Legends|time]] at Echo Base, Chiffonage wore the standard-issue [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|cold-weather fatigues]] of Hoth personnel, including the [[Armor/Legends|uniform's]] [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|padded vest]], [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|lightweight tunic]], [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|leggings]], and [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|boots]]. While working at his control console, he wore [[Alliance Cold Weather armor|gloves]] and a [[Headset comlink|headset]].<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Berryl_Chiffonage.png|thumb|left|250px|Chiffonage in ''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]
The character of Beryl Chiffonage was first created for the [[1988]] [[West End Games]] board game ''[[Assault on Hoth]]'', in which he is established as the innovative Rebel tactician who devises the harpoon and tow cable tactic used during the Battle of Hoth.<ref name="Assault on Hoth" /> Chiffonage was later included in the [[1989]] first edition of the West End Games sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'', which further expounds upon Chiffonage's involvement in the development of the Rebel tactics used at the Battle of Hoth, most notably Attack Pattern Delta and the harpoon and tow cable trick.<ref name="GG3" /> Chiffonage's backstory was detailed in the [[1995]] ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', which established the character as a valuable tactician of both Alliance High Command and Alliance Intelligence.<ref name="AIR" />

The [[February 24]], [[2004]]<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=community/news/2004/01/news20040126.html|text=Clone Trooper Front and Center in ''Insider'' #74}}</ref> article [[Who's Who in Echo Base: The Unsung Rebels of the Battle of Hoth|''Who's Who in Echo Base'']], written by [[Josh Radke]] for [[Star Wars Insider 74|''Star Wars Insider'' 74]], identifies Chiffonage as one of the Rebel extras present in the Echo Base command center working with Princess Leia Organa at the beginning of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], [[Retroactive continuity|retconning]] the film as the character's first appearance.<ref name="Who's Who" /> The scenes in the Echo Base control room featuring the character retconned as Chiffonage were shot on Stage 1 at [[Elstree Studios]] between [[March 26]] and [[March 28|28]], [[1979]].<ref name="MakingOf">''[[The Making of The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref>

===Inconsistencies===
''Assault on Hoth'' originally established Chiffonage as a him,<ref name="Assault on Hoth" /> but the subsequent creation of ''Alliance Intelligence Reports'' contradicted this by identifying the character as a her, the only source to do so.<ref name="AIR" /> Radke's ''Who's Who'' article identifies Chiffonage as him—one of the real-life actors seen in ''The Empire Strikes Back''<ref name="Who's Who" />—as does ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', released in [[2008]].<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|123|Chiffonage, Beryl}}</ref> Given the majority of sources, this article treats ''Alliance Intelligence Reports'' as a [[canon]]ical inconsistency.

Im [[2014]], Chiffonage was included in ''[[Star Wars in 100 Scenes]]''. That source had his given name with the spelling of "Berryl," and also used an image of the character [[Romas Navander/Legends|Romas Navander]] instead of Chiffonage.<ref name="100Scenes">''[[Star Wars in 100 Scenes]]''</ref>
